fields.name: Xcent
fields.description: Compact and fuel-efficient sedan, ideal for city and short pilgrimage trips.

fields.description: Spacious sedan with ample boot space for luggage-heavy trips.

fields.name: Lodgy
fields.description: Roomy SUV with flexible seating for small family groups.

fields.name: Ertiga
fields.description: Popular MPV offering great comfort for family trips.

fields.name: Tavera
fields.description: Sturdy SUV suited for longer group journeys and hill routes.

fields.name: Tourister Non AC
fields.description: Large capacity non-AC coach for big groups and pilgrimages.

fields.name: Ciaz
fields.description: Premium sedan with plush interiors for a smooth, quiet ride.

fields.name: Marazzo
fields.description: Premium MPV with generous legroom and modern comfort features.

fields.name: Innova
fields.description: India's most trusted premium SUV for family and business travel.

fields.name: Crysta
fields.description: Top-tier SUV with luxury seating and superior ride quality.

fields.name: Tempo Traveller 14+1 AC
fields.description: AC tempo traveller with pushback seats for medium groups.

fields.name: Tempo Traveller 18+1 AC

fields.name: Rameswaram
fields.description: Home to the sacred Ramanathaswamy Temple and the iconic Pamban Bridge, Rameswaram is one of the holiest pilgrimage sites in India, with pristine beaches nearby.

fields.name: Madurai
fields.description: Famous for the ancient Meenakshi Amman Temple, Madurai is a vibrant temple city rich in culture, history and South Indian cuisine.

fields.name: Dhanushkodi
fields.description: A ghost town at the tip of the Indian peninsula where the Bay of Bengal meets the Indian Ocean — perfect for scenic day trips.

fields.name: Kanyakumari
fields.description: The southernmost tip of India, known for stunning sunrise and sunset views over three converging seas.

fields.name: Thanjavur
fields.description: Home to the UNESCO World Heritage Brihadeeswarar Temple and a rich legacy of Chola-era art and architecture.

fields.name: Kutralam
fields.description: Known as the 'Spa of South India', famous for its therapeutic waterfalls best visited during monsoon.

fields.name: Kodaikanal
fields.description: A serene hill station with misty lakes, pine forests and pleasant weather — ideal for a romantic getaway.

fields.name: Ooty
fields.description: The 'Queen of Hill Stations' offering tea gardens, botanical gardens and a toy train experience.

fields.name: Munnar
fields.description: Rolling tea plantations and cool climate make Munnar a favourite for couples and nature lovers.

fields.name: Yercaud
fields.description: A quiet hill station perfect for group getaways with coffee estates and scenic viewpoints.

fields.name: Coonoor
fields.description: A quieter alternative to Ooty with sweeping viewpoints, tea estates and colonial-era charm — great for couples.

fields.name: Yelagiri
fields.description: A lesser-known hill station with a scenic lake, trekking trails and adventure sports, ideal for group trips.

fields.name: Valparai
fields.description: A misty plateau in the Western Ghats known for hairpin roads, wildlife sightings and tea plantations.

fields.name: Palani
fields.description: One of the six abodes of Lord Murugan, Palani is a major pilgrimage hill temple reached by rope-car or foot.

fields.name: Alleppey
fields.description: Known as the 'Venice of the East', Alleppey's backwaters and houseboat cruises make it a favourite romantic escape.

fields.name: Kumarakom
fields.description: A tranquil backwater village on Vembanad Lake, famous for houseboats, bird sanctuaries and Ayurvedic resorts.

fields.name: Varkala
fields.description: A dramatic cliffside beach town with sea-facing cafes and sunset views, perfect for a relaxed couples trip.

fields.name: Vagamon
fields.description: Rolling green meadows, pine forests and mist-covered hills make Vagamon a peaceful monsoon retreat.

fields.name: Ponmudi
fields.description: A quiet hill station near Thiruvananthapuram with winding roads, waterfalls and lush monsoon greenery.

fields.name: Thekkady
fields.description: Home to Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary, Thekkady offers boat safaris, spice plantations and forest treks for the whole family.

fields.name: Kovalam
fields.description: A popular crescent beach destination near Thiruvananthapuram, known for its lighthouse and lively beachside cafes.

fields.name: Wayanad
fields.description: A biodiversity-rich hill district with trekking trails, caves, waterfalls and wildlife sanctuaries.

fields.name: Athirapally
fields.description: Often called the 'Niagara of India', these thundering waterfalls are at their most spectacular during monsoon.

fields.name: Idukki
fields.description: Known for its massive arch dam and lush hill terrain, Idukki is a scenic monsoon destination for family trips.

fields.name: Coorg
fields.description: Known as the 'Scotland of India', Coorg's coffee plantations and cool climate make it a top romantic hill getaway.

fields.name: Chikmagalur
fields.description: Rolling coffee estates and misty peaks make Chikmagalur a scenic monsoon retreat, especially for couples.

fields.name: Mysore
fields.description: Famous for the illuminated Mysore Palace, Dasara festivities and sprawling gardens — a great family heritage trip.

fields.name: Gokarna
fields.description: A laid-back temple town with unspoilt beaches, popular with groups looking for a relaxed coastal getaway.

fields.name: Hampi
fields.description: A UNESCO World Heritage site with sprawling ancient ruins, boulder-strewn landscapes and rock climbing spots.

fields.name: Bandipur
fields.description: A renowned tiger reserve offering jeep safaris and wildlife spotting amid the Western Ghats foothills.

fields.name: Kotagiri
fields.description: The oldest and quietest of the Nilgiri hill stations, known for tea gardens, waterfalls and cool weather.

fields.name: Marari Beach
fields.description: A peaceful Kerala fishing village beach, lush and green through monsoon, ideal for a quiet family break.

fields.name: Meghamalai
fields.description: Known as the 'High Wavy Mountains', this offbeat Western Ghats retreat is a favourite for group trekking trips.

fields.title: Aslam — Founder & Managing Director
fields.content: With over 10 years in the travel industry, Selvakumar built Dreams Tours and Travels on a simple promise: every traveller reaches home safely, comfortably, and on time. What began as a single sedan has grown into a trusted fleet serving thousands of families and pilgrims across South India.

fields.caption: A Beautiful Travel ends with Dreams Tours and Travel.
A blessed journey to Ramanathaswamy, Faith on the road

fields.review_text:
Aslam ji agreed to provide 12 seater for our religious trip to South India. We hired tempo traveller (12 seater) for three days from kanyakumari-rameshwaram-madurai-trichy. In these four days, we went to rameshwaram, dhanuskodi, bibhishan pattaveshakam, pamban beach, meenakshi temple (madurai), sri ranganathswamy temple (trichy). Drivers (Sachin until madurai and Bhumi until Trichy) knew the places of attractions in between.
Big thanks to Aslam

I Booked with DREAMS TOURS & TRAVELS for vacation.. Mr.ASLAM such a elegant and good communicated person he arranged everything for us and Mr.SIDDIQ calm and good responsible driver.... its wondreful travel with DREAMS T&TRAVELS .... BEST FOR FAMILY VACATION.
I have travelled in and around Rameshwaram with Dreams tourism along with Aslam it was nice and pleasant remarkable trip. The way he talked is very polite
Supper memories, Mr. Thiru APJ Abdul Kalam Home and Shopping Complex. Dhanushkodi Light House and Near Restaurant, Dhanushkodi End Of the Innagarat Pillar With Beaches.

I am very happy with the cab driver Aslam from Dreams Tour's and Travels. I booked the cab from Mandapam to Rameswaram trip. He is so polite in receiving the people, very humble and decent throughout the Rameswaram trip. He is never forced to rush and waits still site seeing finishes. I highly recommend him for any one who wants to go around the trip.

fields.title: Rameswaram Temple Guide: Timings, Rituals and What to Expect
fields.excerpt: Everything you need to know before visiting the Ramanathaswamy Temple — timings, rituals, dress code and insider tips for a smooth pilgrimage.
fields.content:
The Ramanathaswamy Temple in Rameswaram is one of the twelve Jyotirlinga shrines and a key stop on the Char Dham pilgrimage circuit. If it's your first visit, a little planning goes a long way.

Temple Timings

The temple opens early morning and closes for a short break in the afternoon before reopening for evening darshan. Timings can shift slightly during festivals, so it's worth confirming on the day of your visit.

The 22 Holy Wells

Inside the temple complex are 22 theerthams (holy wells), each believed to have different healing properties. Pilgrims traditionally bathe in all 22 before entering for darshan. Priests are usually available to guide you through this ritual for a small fee.

Dress Code

Modest clothing is expected — avoid shorts, sleeveless tops, or anything considered inappropriate for a place of worship. Men are sometimes asked to remove shirts before entering the inner sanctum, which is a long-standing local custom.

Best Time to Visit

October to March offers the most pleasant weather for temple visits and combines well with a Dhanushkodi day trip. Early mornings are quieter and cooler than midday.

Planning a pilgrimage trip to Rameswaram? We arrange comfortable, on-time transport so you can focus on the experience, not the logistics.
fields.author: Dreams Tours and Travels
fields.meta_title: Rameswaram Temple Guide: Timings, Rituals & Tips | Dreams Tours

fields.title: Sedan vs SUV vs Tempo Traveller: Which Should You Book?
fields.excerpt: Choosing the right vehicle for your trip comes down to group size, luggage, and route. Here's a simple breakdown to help you decide.
fields.content:
One of the most common questions we get is which vehicle to book. Here's a quick guide based on group size and travel style.

Sedans (4+1 seater)

Best for couples, solo travellers, or small families of up to 4. Sedans are fuel-efficient, comfortable on highways, and the most budget-friendly option for short pilgrimage circuits like Rameswaram to Madurai.

SUVs (6+1 to 7+1 seater)

Ideal for families of 5-7 or friend groups wanting extra luggage space and a higher ride height — especially useful on hill routes like Kodaikanal or Munnar.

Tempo Travellers (14+1 to 18+1 seater)

The go-to choice for larger groups, extended families, or pilgrimage groups travelling together. AC tempo travellers with pushback seats make long journeys far more comfortable for bigger parties.

Our Recommendation

If you're unsure, tell us your group size, luggage, and route when you enquire — we're happy to recommend the best fit rather than the most expensive option.
fields.author: Dreams Tours and Travels
fields.meta_title: Sedan vs SUV vs Tempo Traveller — Which to Book? | Dreams Tours

fields.title: Dhanushkodi: How to Plan Your Trip to India's Ghost Town
fields.excerpt: Once a thriving port town, Dhanushkodi is now a hauntingly beautiful stretch where two seas meet. Here's how to plan your visit.
fields.content:
Dhanushkodi sits at the very tip of the Rameswaram peninsula, where the Bay of Bengal meets the Indian Ocean. Abandoned after a devastating cyclone in 1964, it's now one of South India's most striking day-trip destinations.

Getting There

Dhanushkodi is roughly 18 km from Rameswaram town. The last stretch of road runs along a narrow strip of sand with water visible on both sides — an experience in itself.

What to See

The ruined church and railway station are reminders of the town before the cyclone. Further ahead, the point where the two seas visibly meet is one of the most photographed spots in the region.

Best Time to Go

Early morning or late afternoon avoids the midday heat and gives the best light for photos. November to February is the most comfortable season to visit.

Good to Know

There are very limited food and water facilities on the way, so carry enough for the trip. A local vehicle familiar with the route makes the journey much smoother, especially on the sandy final stretch.
fields.author: Dreams Tours and Travels
